flow_yaml:
name: my-first-flow
steps:
  fetch:
    run: |
      echo '{"data": "hello world"}'
    outputs: [data]

  summarize:
    run: |
      echo "{\"summary\": \"processed: $data\"}"
    inputs:
      data: fetch.data
    outputs: [summary]

confusion_points.description: README doesn't directly show a minimal script-only flow — the quickstart example uses agent and external executors which require API keys
confusion_points.how_resolved: Found flows/rapid-test.flow.yaml which shows a minimal script step with echo + JSON

confusion_points.description: Not immediately clear how shell variables from inputs are injected — is it $data or $STEPWISE_INPUT_DATA?
confusion_points.how_resolved: README code-review example shows $var syntax in run blocks (e.g. $diff_summary), and the welcome flow confirms this pattern extensively

confusion_points.description: README quickstart section says 'Create code-review.flow.yaml' but doesn't mention what directory to put it in or whether stepwise init is needed first
confusion_points.how_resolved: Tried running from /tmp and it worked fine — no .stepwise/ directory required for basic flows

confusion_points.description: No simple hello-world or two-step script tutorial in quickstart — jumps straight to a 4-step flow with agent and external executors
confusion_points.how_resolved: Pieced together the pattern from rapid-test.flow.yaml and the parallel-research example

evidence.requirement: README explains how to create a flow
evidence.evidence: README.md has inline code-review.flow.yaml example and links to docs/quickstart.md which has a 'Create your own flow' section with a complete walkthrough

Quickstart explains 'Dependencies are implicit from inputs:'. The code-review example shows 'diff_summary: gather-context.diff_summary' syntax. $job.field syntax shown in quickstart --input example and welcome flow ($job.team_name)
Both README and quickstart examples show 'outputs: [field1, field2]' syntax on every step definition

evidence.requirement: stepwise validate is documented as a way to check flows
evidence.evidence: README CLI section lists 'stepwise validate <flow>   Check a flow for errors'

evidence.requirement: At least one complete example flow is available to reference
evidence.evidence: Multiple example flows available: flows/welcome/FLOW.yaml (comprehensive), flows/rapid-test.flow.yaml (minimal), examples/report-test-parallel.flow.yaml (fan-out pattern)

evidence.requirement: The flow you built validates and would run correctly
evidence.evidence: Flow passed 'stepwise validate' with no errors/warnings, and ran successfully with 'stepwise run' completing both steps in 0.1s
